title: Issue of Surface Waves on Water in High School Laboratory Workshops
author: Chkhartishvili L
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This study addresses the gap in traditional General Physics courses by introducing a laboratory experiment to measure the group velocity of circular surface waves on water. Unlike the usual qualitative observations, this experiment allows for quantitative analysis using a water tank and wave generator, optimized for clear wave representations. Key innovations include adjustable generator amplitude for enhanced wave contrast and precise magnification calculations for measuring group wavelength. Tests demonstrated a high measurement accuracy, making this method suitable for technical university curricula. Incorporating such experiments enhances students' understanding of wave phenomena in engineering education.
